title: Ensure that the --seccomp-default parameter is set to true

input_row.title: Ensure that the --seccomp-default parameter is set to true
input_row.description: Ensure that the Kubelet enforces the use of the RuntimeDefault seccomp profile
input_row.rationale: By default, Kubernetes disables the seccomp profile which ships with most container runtimes. Setting this parameter will ensure workloads running on the node are protected by the runtime's seccomp profile. Impact: Setting this will remove some rights from pods running on the node.
input_row.audit: Review the Kubelet's start-up parameters for the value of --seccomp-default, and check the Kubelet configuration file for the seccompDefault . If neither of these values is set, then the seccomp profile is not in use.
input_row.remediation: Set the parameter, either via the --seccomp-default command line parameter or the seccompDefault configuration file setting. Default Value: By default the seccomp profile is not enabled. References: 1. https://kubernetes.io/docs/tutorials/security/seccomp/#enable-the-use-of- runtimedefault-as-the-default-seccomp-profile-for-all-workloads
input_row.impact: Setting this will remove some rights from pods running on the node.

step1_initial_assessment:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**PROGRAM NAME:** k8s_kubelet_seccomp_default_profile_enabled

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- Query the Kubelet configuration on each worker node to verify that the seccomp default profile is enabled.
- Specifically, check for either:
  - The presence of the `--seccomp-default=true` flag in the Kubelet process arguments, OR
  - The `seccompDefault: true` setting in the Kubelet configuration file (typically `/var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml`).
- This can be automated by:
  - Using `kubectl get node` to enumerate all nodes.
  - For each node, use `kubectl describe node <node-name>` to check for the `kubelet` process configuration if exposed via node annotations (rare).
  - More reliably, use a DaemonSet to run an inspection pod on each node that reads the Kubelet config file (`/var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml`) and/or inspects the running process arguments for `--seccomp-default=true`.
  - Alternatively, if your environment exposes Kubelet config via the Node API (as in some managed Kubernetes distributions), query the `KubeletConfiguration` custom resource or node annotation.
- **Pass Criteria:** At least one of the following is true for every node:
  - The Kubelet is started with `--seccomp-default=true`
  - The Kubelet config file contains `seccompDefault: true`
- **Fail Criteria:** If neither the flag nor the config file setting is present or set to `false` on any node.
- **Example CLI commands:**
  - To check via SSH (if allowed): `ps aux | grep kubelet` and `cat /var/lib/kubelet/config.yaml`
  - To automate in-cluster: Deploy a privileged DaemonSet that checks these settings and reports compliance status per node.

**SOURCE:** CIS KUBERNETES BENCHMARK V1.11 PDF
